mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of observations where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth during periods of elevated inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in over 60 percent of high-inflation periods following INDPRO acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 220 signal entrenched inflation that forces monetary policy tightening, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns show a positive mean when CPIAUCSL exceeds 220, contradicting the inflation-tightening transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 60-day S&P 500 returns falls below 0.02, indicating no persistent predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while real personal consumption expenditures accelerate indicates a shift toward consumer-driven demand that outperforms broader economic output, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS growth divergence fails to predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction during a monetary easing cycle signals severe supply-side weakness that prompts aggressive policy accommodation, rapidly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of observations where INDPRO declines during FEDFUNDS cuts.
